title: codariocalyx microphyllus f. glaber
intro: Codariocalyx microphyllus f. glaber, also known as climbing milk vine, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Leguminosae and is native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant are used in herbal medicine, exhibiting analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Codariocalyx microphyllus f. glaber include its potential to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its ability to treat wounds and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional African medicine to treat various ailments.
